text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both academic and professional settings. Good time management starts with breaking a large project into smaller, manageable tasks and setting mini-deadlines for each. Prioritization is equally important—focus on high-impact activities first before moving to less critical ones. Scope management involves clearly defining what the task includes and avoiding unnecessary additions, as extra work can derail your schedule. Finally, open communication with team members or supervisors is key. If you foresee a delay, inform others early to adjust expectations or resources. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines with less stress and better results.
